overview: A flexible G/U/W midrange/control hybrid that leverages Ms. Bumbleflower as a versatile engine for mana acceleration, token generation, and anti-token disruption. Wins by out-valuing opponents through efficient removal, card advantage, and strategic Saproling floods that overwhelm board states or enable favorable sweepers.
win_conditions: Bumbleflower token floods combined with board sweepers, or a late-game resilient threat protected by Saprolings.
Card advantage engines that out-value opponents and force unfavorable trades.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fetches/shocks if in deck), or 1 land plus 2+ mana accelerants (Arcane Signet, Sol Ring, etc.).
Keep hands with turn-2 interaction (Swords to Plowshares, early removal, or countermagic) and a turn-3 play (Ms.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, pure curve decks, or no early interaction/mana sources.

early_game: Turn 2: Cast a 2-drop or Arcane Signet.
Sequence mana carefully: if ahead, tap for G/U to develop threats
if behind, consider early sacrifice to flood the board with Saprolings for blocking or to enable removal.
mid_game: Bumbleflower for both mana generation and token generation
avoid overcommitting to Saproling floods without removal or sweepers ready.
Use Swords to Plowshares efficiently on key threats, and manage hand resources to maintain interaction through the midgame.
Commit threats when you have removal or sweepers ready to handle their response.
Hold back if they have heavy interaction or sweepers in hand.
Use Saprolings as flexible blockers or to trade favorably with key creatures, preserving your board state for value plays.
late_game: Bumbleflower token floods combined with board sweepers, or a late-game resilient threat protected by Saprolings.
Card advantage engines that out-value opponents and force unfavorable trades.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, removal for their threats, and they lack sweepers.
Commit when you can safely flood the board knowing you can clear it or trade favorably, or when you can close the game before they stabilize.

sequencing_tips: Bumbleflower too early without a plan, overcommitting to token floods without removal, and ignoring opponent mana states.
Bumbleflower's sacrifice triggers go on the stack, allowing responses.
Synergizes well with token doublers, sweepers, and card draw.
Watch for sacrifice hate or destroy effects, and remember that Saprolings can be used to enable sacrifice outlets or block key creatures.

matchups.advice: Use mana rocks to cast spells at instant speed when they are tapped out. Avoid overcommitting to single large spells; instead, use smaller threats, token floods, and split strategies to overwhelm counters.
Apply early pressure and use removal on their mana sources. Do not let them stabilize; trade efficiently and force them to spend resources on board development rather than acceleration.
Prioritize early removal, life gain, and using Saprolings as blockers. Hold back Ms. Bumbleflower for mana or blocking until needed, and stabilize before attempting to go off.
Pressure with resilient threats and card draw to out-value. Force them to use removal on low-value targets, and use token floods to clog their board and enable favorable trades.

Value exchange is paramount. Use targeted removal on their key pieces, out-value with superior token synergies, and control the board with well-timed sweepers. Prioritize card advantage and hand disruption to out-resource them.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath, Cyclonic), targeted removal (Swords, Go for the Throat), and combo wincons.
Note when opponents are holding up mana for counterspells or instant-speed interaction.
Track graveyard for reanimation or recursion threats, and monitor life totals for aggressive pushes.
